title: Ghostty GTK split-tree flicker — 6 months unsolved, cracked by Codex 5.3 (Opus 4.6 failed)
crash_site: (no crash — a visual flicker; there is no error string to grep)

ground_truth.decoy_fn: (none forced; the renderer / surface widgets are plausible misdirections)
ground_truth.fix_summary: stop clearing tree_bin before the async rebuild; debounce + keep old tree visible, swap the bin child atomically to the new root, and reuse existing leaf SurfaceScrolledWindow widgets (reparent) instead of recreating them

admission.note: Guaranteed discriminating: open ~6 months; the human author + Opus 4.6 + lower Codex levels all failed. Only Codex 5.3 (xhigh) solved it. Run baseline for the record anyway.

provenance: GitHub issue #8208 'gtk-ng: fix split change (new, delete, resize, etc.) flicker', closed 2026-03-04. Solved by Codex 5.3 (xhigh) with a vague prompt in 45 min / $4.14 after Mitchell Hashimoto + others struggled 6+ months; Opus 4.6 and other Codex reasoning levels failed (https://x.com/mitchellh/status/2029348087538565612). Fix commit 264dbf9e; agent trace at ampcode.com/threads/T-019cbadf-cb5a-742e-b0e3-2d7164de743f.
